payload:
# Minimal Kanban Board Specification

## Component Structure
- Single HTML file (index.html)
- Three main sections: header, board container, column containers
- Board contains exactly 3 columns: Todo, Doing, Done
- Each column has a card list and an "Add Card" input area

## Data Model
```json
{
  "board": {
    "columns": [
      {
        "id": "todo",
        "title": "Todo",
        "cards": [
          {"id": "card-uuid", "title": "Card title text"}
        ]
      },
      {"id": "doing", "title": "Doing", "cards": []},
      {"id": "done", "title": "Done", "cards": []}
    ]
  }
}
```

## User-Facing Behaviors
1. **View board**: Load persisted state from localStorage key "kanban-board" on page load
2. **Add card**: Text input + button per column; creates card with unique ID and title
3. **Move card**: Drag-drop or move buttons (prev/next column) on each card
4. **Delete card**: Delete button on each card removes it permanently
5. **Persist**: Save full board state to localStorage after every mutation

## Interface Contract (localStorage)
- Key: "kanban-board"
- Value: JSON string of board object above
- Write on: add card, move card, delete card
- Read on: page load only

## Styling Notes
- Flexbox layout, 3 equal-width columns
- Cards are rounded boxes with shadow, readable padding
- Buttons are small, clearly labeled
- No animations or transitions required

HTML is valid, 3+ columns present, cards can be added and moved (via Prev/Next buttons), delete works, localStorage persists state across reload, backend API is properly wired and callable, and no JS errors detected. Ready for delivery."}
